commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fa2266bf8634c997d7a3f9f7687461ef96cfefb4)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a non-CLI full-reindex trigger when memory index identity is missing while chunks exist, plus a new condition-focused test file for that guard and identity-state resolution.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +146. Total +157 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main returns early on missing identity with indexed chunks and search returns [] for invalid identity; the existing missing-meta test documents that current behavior. I did not execute live tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied inspectable real behavior proof plus maintainer coordination with https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/90727; automation cannot supply the contributor's live-gateway proof.

target.title: fix(memory): self-heal missing index identity during sync
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91167.

A long-running gateway whose memory index-identity becomes `"missing"` while chunks
are already indexed never recovered: `search()` gates on a valid identity and returns
`[]`, and `sync()` only rebuilt a chunk-backed invalid index for `reason === "cli"`
(`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts`). The only repair was an
out-of-process `openclaw memory` reindex, which atomically swaps the DB file
(`manager-atomic-reindex.ts`) and orphans the running gateway's open inode — so the
CLI "fix" never reached the live process and a restart was required.

This adds an in-process self-heal: when the identity is `"missing"` and chunks exist,
an ordinary sync rebuilds via `runSafeReindex`, which reopens the DB handle through
`openDatabase()` — recovering both the lost metadata and any stale/orphaned handle.
A deliberate provider/model change (`"mismatched"`) stays operator-gated.

## Real Behavior Proof

Tested on live gateway with patched code. Steps:

1. Delete index identity meta from live database:
   - `sqlite3 ~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite "DELETE FROM meta WHERE key='memory_index_meta_v1';"`
   - Meta rows: 0, Chunks: 1953 (unchanged)
2. Wait for next sync cycle (~20 seconds)
3. Verify meta was restored:
   - Meta rows: 1 (correctly populated)
4. Memory search works again (no "index metadata is missing" warning)

Without fix: same scenario results in `Memory index warning: index metadata is missing` indefinitely.
Only recovery was manual CLI reindex + gateway restart.

## Verification

- Manual repro verified on affected system before/after patch
- Unit tests added for needsSelfHealReindex condition logic + identity state resolution

## Not covered (follow-up)

Reopen-on-swap for a stale-but-`"valid"` identity after a concurrent CLI file swap
(Defect B in the issue) — tracked separately; touches the multi-process swap contract.
